steps.title: Identify what providing an example means
steps.content: The goal is to provide an example of an air mass — a specific, named instance, not a definition or a description of the classification system. The notes supply two ready examples: cA (continental arctic, dry and cold) and mT (maritime tropical, moist and warm). A correct answer names one of these and explains what it means.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Air masses are large bodies of air with generally uniform humidity and temperature.': States the general definition of an air mass; gives no specific example. ✗
  • Choice B — 'The air mass classification system uses two-letter names and is based on the work of Tor Bergeron, a Swedish meteorologist.': Describes the classification system and its origin; no specific air mass is named. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Air masses are commonly classified by a two-letter name that indicates humidity and temperature.': Explains how classification works in general; still no concrete example. ✗
  • Choice D — 'One type of air mass is known as a cA, or continental arctic, air mass because it is dry and cold.': Names the specific cA air mass and explains what it means — a textbook example. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
